commands.name: init
commands.summary: Initialize CipherStash for your project
commands.long:
Set up CipherStash end-to-end: authenticate, introspect your database,
install dependencies, install EQL, and hand off the rest to your local
coding agent. Every prompt has a non-interactive escape hatch, so init
never blocks waiting on a TTY (CI, agents, pipes).

flags.name: --supabase
flags.description: Use Supabase-specific setup flow.

flags.name: --drizzle
flags.description: Use Drizzle-specific setup flow.

flags.name: --prisma-next
flags.description: Use Prisma Next-specific setup flow (EQL bundle installed via prisma-next migration apply).

flags.name: --proxy
flags.description: Query encrypted data via CipherStash Proxy.

flags.name: --no-proxy
flags.description: Query encrypted data directly via the SDK.

flags.name: --region
flags.description: Region to authenticate against (e.g. us-east-1). Skips the interactive region picker. Required for non-interactive init when not already logged in.

commands.name: plan
commands.summary: Draft a reviewable encryption plan at .cipherstash/plan.md

flags.name: --complete-rollout
flags.description: Plan the entire encryption lifecycle (schema-add through drop) in one document. Skips the production-deploy gate; only safe when this database is not backing a deployed application.

flags.name: --target
flags.description: Skip the agent-target picker and hand off directly to one of claude-code | codex | agents-md | wizard. Safe in non-TTY contexts.

commands.name: impl
commands.summary: Execute the plan with a local agent

flags.name: --continue-without-plan
flags.description: Skip planning and go straight to implementation (interactively confirms before proceeding).

flags.name: --target
flags.description: Skip the agent-target picker and hand off directly to one of claude-code | codex | agents-md | wizard. Safe in non-TTY contexts.

commands.name: status
commands.summary: Displays implementation status

flags.name: --quest
flags.description: Force the quest-log output (emoji + progress bars) even in non-TTY contexts.

flags.name: --plain
flags.description: Force the plain-text output even in TTY contexts.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit a structured JSON document instead.

commands.name: wizard
commands.summary: AI-guided encryption setup (reads your codebase)

commands.name: doctor
commands.summary: Diagnose install problems (native binaries, runtime)

commands.name: manifest
commands.summary: Print the structured, versioned command surface
commands.long:
Emit the CLI command surface as data. `--json` produces the machine-
readable manifest the docs generator and agents consume; without it a
grouped command list is printed. The manifest is stamped with the CLI
version, so a page generated from it always names the version it describes.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit the structured JSON manifest instead of a text list.

commands.name: auth login
commands.summary: Authenticate with CipherStash
commands.long:
Runs the OAuth 2.0 device authorization flow:
1. Pick a region for your workspace.
2. Approve in the browser — the URL is printed, so it works over SSH/headless.
3. The CLI polls until you approve, then stores a short-lived token.
4. Your device is bound to the workspace's default keyset, so later
   commands authenticate without a fresh login.

flags.name: --region
flags.description: Region to authenticate against (e.g. us-east-1). Skips the interactive region picker.

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit newline-delimited JSON events instead of prose. The first event (authorization_required) carries the device verification URL for a human to open. Implies no prompt and no browser auto-open.

flags.name: --no-open
flags.description: Don't auto-open the verification URL in a browser (already implied by --json).

flags.name: --supabase
flags.description: Track Supabase as the referrer.

flags.name: --drizzle
flags.description: Track Drizzle as the referrer.

commands.name: auth regions
commands.summary: List the regions you can authenticate against

flags.name: --json
flags.description: Emit machine-readable [{ slug, label }] instead of a text list.

commands.name: eql install
commands.summary: Scaffold stash.config.ts (if missing) and install EQL extensions

flags.name: --force
flags.description: Reinstall / overwrite even if already installed.

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Show what would happen without making changes.

flags.name: --supabase
flags.description: Use Supabase-compatible mode (auto-detected from DATABASE_URL).

flags.name: --drizzle
flags.description: Generate a Drizzle migration instead of direct install (auto-detected from project).

flags.name: --migration
flags.description: Write a Supabase migration file instead of running SQL directly (requires --supabase).

flags.name: --direct
flags.description: Run the SQL directly against the database (requires --supabase; mutually exclusive with --migration).

flags.name: --migrations-dir
flags.description: Override the Supabase migrations directory (requires --supabase).

flags.name: --exclude-operator-family
flags.description: Skip operator family creation.

flags.name: --eql-version
flags.description: EQL generation to target. v3 is the native eql_v3.* domain schema (direct install only for now).

flags.name: --latest
flags.description: Fetch the latest EQL from GitHub (v2 only).

flags.name: --name
flags.description: With --drizzle: name for the generated migration (defaults to a scaffold name).

flags.name: --out
flags.description: With --drizzle: directory to write the generated migration into.

flags.name: --database-url
flags.description: Override DATABASE_URL for this run only — never written to disk.
